title: प्रह्लाद स्तुति
devanagariText:
॥ श्री प्रह्लाद स्तुति ॥

प्रह्लाद उवाच —
ब्रह्मादयः सुरगणा मुनयोऽथ सिद्धाः
सत्त्वैकतानगतयो वचसां प्रवाहैः।
नाराधितुं पुरुगुणैरधुनापि पिप्रुः
किं तोष्टुमर्हति स मे हरिरुग्रजातेः॥ १॥

मन्ये धनाभिजनरूपतपःश्रुतौजः-
तेजःप्रभावबलपौरुषबुद्धियोगाः।
नाराधनाय हि भवन्ति परस्य पुंसो
भक्त्या तुतोष भगवान् गजयूथपाय॥ २॥

विप्राद्द्विषड्गुणयुतादरविन्दनाभ-
पादारविन्दविमुखाच्छ्वपचं वरिष्ठम्।
मन्ये तदर्पितमनोवचनेहिताऽर्थ-
प्राणं पुनाति स कुलं न तु भूरिमानः॥ ३॥

nैवात्मनः प्रभुरयं निजलाभपूर्णो
मानं जनादविदुषः करुणो वृणीते।
यद्यज्जनो भगवते विदधीत मानं
तच्चात्मने प्रतिमुखस्य यथा मुखश्रीः॥ ४॥

तस्मादहं विगतविक्लव ईश्वरस्य
सर्वात्मना मह उपैमि शरण्यमेकम्।
यत् प्रेतमात्ममनसात्मवता प्रपन्न-
मक्ष्णोर्विभक्तिमदनुग्रह ईशतुभ्यम्॥ ५॥

बालस्य नेह शरणं पितरौ नृसिंह
नार्तस्य चागदमुदन्वति मज्जतो नौः।
तप्तस्य तत्प्रतिविधिर्य इहाञ्जसेष्ट-
स्तावद्विभो तनुभृतां त्वदुपेक्षितानाम्॥ ६॥

यस्मात्प्रियाप्रियवियोगसंयोगजन्म-
शोकाग्निना सकलयोनिषु दह्यमानः।
दुःखौषधं तदपि दुःखमतद्धियाहं
भूमन् भ्रमामि वद मे तव दास्यतः किम्॥ ७॥

सोऽहं प्रियस्य सुहृदः परदेवतायाः
लीलाकथास्तव नृसिंह विरिञ्चगीताः।
अञ्जस्तितर्म्यनुगृणन्गुणविप्रमुक्तो
दुर्गाणि ते पदयुगालयहंससंगः॥ ८॥

॥ इति श्रीमद्भागवतपुराणे सप्तमस्कन्धे प्रह्लादस्तुतिः सम्पूर्णा ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Prahlada Stuti ||

Prahlada Uvacha —
Brahma-adayah sura-ganah munayo-atha siddhah
Sattva-ekatana-gatayo vachasam pravahaih.
Na-aradhitum puru-gunair-adhuna-api pipruh
Kim toshtum-arhati sa me Harir-ugra-jateh. || 1 ||

Manye dhana-abhijana-rupa-tapah-shruta-ojah-
Tejah-prabhava-bala-paurusha-buddhi-yogah.
Na-aradhanaya hi bhavanti parasya pumso
Bhaktya tutosha Bhagavan gaja-yutha-paya. || 2 ||

Viprad-dvi-shad-guna-yutad-aravinda-nabha-
Pada-aravinda-vimukhach-chva-pacham varishtham.
Manye tad-arpita-mano-vachana-ihita-artha-
Pranam punati sa kulam na tu bhuri-manah. || 3 ||

Naiva-atmanah prabhur-ayam nija-labha-purno
Manam janad-avidusah karuno vrinite.
Yad-yaj-jano Bhagavate vidadhita manam
Tach-cha-atmane prati-mukhasya yatha mukha-shrih. || 4 ||

Tasmad-aham vigata-viklava Ishvarasya
Sarva-atmana maha upaimi sharanyam-ekam.
Yat preta-atma-manasa-atma-vata prapannam-
Akshnor-vibhaktim-adanugraha Isha-tubhyam. || 5 ||

Balasya neha sharanam pitarau Nrisimha
Na-artasya cha-agadam-udanvati majjato nauh.
Taptasya tat-pratividhir-ya iha-anjasa-ishtas-
Tavad-vibho tanu-bhritam tvad-upekshitanam. || 6 ||

Yasmat-priya-apriya-viyoga-samyoga-janma-
Shoka-agnina sakala-yonishu dahya-manah.
Duhkha-aushadham tad-api duhkham-atad-dhiya-aham
Bhuman bhramami vada me tava dasyatah kim. || 7 ||

So-aham priyasya suhridah para-devata-yah
Lila-kathas-tava Nrisimha Virincha-gitah.
Anjas-titarmy-anugrinan-guna-vipra-mukto
Durgani te pada-yugalaya-hamsa-sangah. || 8 ||

|| Iti Shrimad Bhagavata Purane Saptama-Skandhe Prahlada-Stutih Sampurna ||
hindiMeaning:
प्रह्लाद ने कहा — ब्रह्मा आदि देवगण, मुनि और सिद्ध, जो शुद्ध सत्त्व में स्थित हैं, वाणी के प्रवाह से भी भगवान की पूर्ण आराधना नहीं कर सके — तो मुझ उग्रजाति (दैत्यकुल) में जन्मे को वे कैसे प्रसन्न होंगे?॥ १॥

मैं मानता हूँ कि धन, कुल, रूप, तप, विद्या, ओज, तेज, प्रभाव, बल, पराक्रम, बुद्धि और योग — ये सब परमपुरुष की आराधना के साधन नहीं हैं। भगवान तो केवल भक्ति से प्रसन्न हुए — गजेन्द्र के लिए॥ २॥

मैं मानता हूँ कि बारह गुणों से युक्त ब्राह्मण से भी वह चाण्डाल श्रेष्ठ है जिसने अपना मन, वचन, कर्म, धन और प्राण भगवान को अर्पित कर दिया — वह अपने कुल को पवित्र करता है, किन्तु अभिमानी (ऐसा नहीं करता)॥ ३॥

हे नृसिंह! बालक के लिए माता-पिता शरण नहीं हैं, रोगी के लिए औषध (अन्तिम शरण) नहीं, डूबते के लिए नौका (पर्याप्त) नहीं — हे विभो! जब तक आपकी उपेक्षा है, तब तक शरीरधारियों के लिए कोई उपाय काम नहीं करता॥ ६॥

वही मैं, अपने प्रिय सुहृद् परदेवता नृसिंह की ब्रह्मा द्वारा गाई लीलाकथाओं का कीर्तन करता हुआ, गुणों से विप्रमुक्त होकर, आपके चरणयुगल रूपी सरोवर के हंस — सन्तों — के संग से सहज ही दुर्गम संसार को पार कर जाऊँगा॥ ८॥
benefits: Demonstrates that pure bhakti transcends birth, caste, and qualifications
Invokes fearlessness and faith even in the face of extreme adversity
Teaches that God is pleased by devotion alone, not by material qualities
Invokes the blessing of the association of saintly devotees
Protects children and devotees from all dangers
Invokes Lord Narasimha's compassion for the surrendered soul
description: Among the most cherished prayers to Lord Vishnu, Prahlada Stuti (प्रह्लाद स्तुति) stands as a devotional hymn of praise. Attributed to Shrimad Bhagavata Purana, Canto 7, Chapter 9, this prayer holds a special place in Hindu devotional literature. Devotees recite this prayer to demonstrate that pure bhakti transcends birth, caste, and qualifications and protect children and devotees from all dangers. It is also said to grant fearlessness and faith even in the face of extreme adversity. For best results, devotees chant this on Thursday (Guruvar), Sunday (Ravivar), and Saturday (Shanivar) and during Narasimha Jayanti and Holi. Comprising 8 verses, it can be completed in approximately 8 minutes, making it suitable for daily devotional practice. On this page, you can read the complete Prahlada Stuti in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Prahlada Stuti from Bhagavata Purana Canto 7 in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Child devotee Prahlada's prayer to Lord Narasimha.

Narasimha avatar Vishnu lion man form

Prahlada child devotee Vishnu unwavering faith

seoFaqs.question: What is the Prahlada Stuti and where is it preserved?
seoFaqs.answer: The Prahlada Stuti is the famed Sanskrit hymn of praise spoken by the boy-devotee Prahlada when Lord Narasimha — Vishnu in his half-man, half-lion avatar — appeared from the cracking pillar to slay his demon-father Hiranyakashipu and then turned, still raging with cosmic fury, towards the trembling assembly of devas and devotees. While even Brahma, Shiva, Lakshmi and the great rishis dared not approach the blood-stained Lord, the five-year-old Prahlada walked fearlessly to him, bowed, and offered the stuti preserved in the Shrimad Bhagavata Purana, Canto 7, Chapter 9, verses 8 to 50. It is regarded by Vaishnava acharyas as one of the most theologically rich bhakti compositions in all of scripture. (Sources: Bhagavata Purana 7.9; Sridhara Swami commentary; Hindupedia Prahlada.)

seoFaqs.question: Who was Prahlada and what is the story behind this stuti?
seoFaqs.answer: Prahlada was the son of the demon-king Hiranyakashipu, who had obtained from Brahma a near-invincibility boon (immune from death by man or beast, indoors or outdoors, day or night, by any weapon, on earth or in air) and had subsequently terrorised the universe demanding to be worshipped instead of Vishnu. His own son Prahlada, however, became the supreme Vishnu-bhakta — taught in the womb by Sage Narada when Hiranyakashipu had imprisoned his pregnant wife Kayadhu. Despite his father's escalating tortures (poison, snakes, burning by Holika, throwing from cliffs, trampling by elephants, drowning), Prahlada remained protected by Vishnu. Finally Hiranyakashipu challenged whether Vishnu was in a particular pillar, struck it, and Vishnu emerged as Narasimha — neither man nor beast — at twilight, on a threshold, on his lap, with claws — circumventing every clause of the boon. (Sources: Bhagavata Purana 7.2-9; Hindupedia Narasimha avatar.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the spiritual significance of Prahlada's fearless approach to Narasimha?
seoFaqs.answer: After slaying Hiranyakashipu, Narasimha's cosmic rage was so terrible that no being could approach him. Brahma, Shiva, Lakshmi (his own consort), and the assembled devas and rishis stood at a distance, unable to pacify him. It was the small child Prahlada — the very devotee for whose protection Narasimha had descended — who walked forward fearlessly because his bhakti contained no fear. The Bhagavata acharyas extract from this episode the central teaching of saranagati: that the soul completely surrendered to the Lord experiences no fear even of the Lord's most terrifying form, because the Lord and the devotee are one. The stuti that flowed from Prahlada in this moment contains the deepest insights of Vaishnava theology delivered in childlike simplicity. (Sources: Bhagavata Purana 7.9.4-7; Sridhara Swami; Vishvanatha Chakravarti commentary.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the nine forms of bhakti that Prahlada teaches?
seoFaqs.answer: In a separate but related Bhagavata passage (7.5.23), when his father asks what learning he has acquired, Prahlada famously enumerates the nine angas (limbs) of bhakti-yoga: shravanam (hearing of the Lord's glories), kirtanam (chanting them), Vishnu-smaranam (constant remembrance), pada-sevanam (service to his lotus feet), archanam (ritual worship), vandanam (offering prostrations), dasyam (servitude), sakhyam (cultivation of friendship with the Lord), and atma-nivedanam (complete self-surrender). This nine-fold bhakti formulation became the foundational classification used by all later Vaishnava acharyas — Ramanuja, Madhva, Chaitanya, Vallabha — and structures the entire Bhakti Yoga path. The Prahlada Stuti embodies all nine angas in lived demonstration. (Sources: Bhagavata Purana 7.5.23; Hindupedia nine forms of bhakti; Narada Bhakti Sutras.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the famous Tava karakamale vare verse from this stuti?
seoFaqs.answer: Among the most beloved verses of the Prahlada Stuti is Bhagavata 7.9.36, often quoted as Tava karakamala vare nakham adbhuta-shringam — 'O wonder of wonders, those nails of yours, sharp like crescent moons on your beautiful lotus-hand, with which you tore open the body of the king of demons!' The verse, immortalised by Jayadeva in the Dasavatara stotra of the Gita Govinda (Tava karakamale dharatu khedam khalalupita-tata-bhuvana-gata-pada Keshava dhrita-Narahari-rupa Jaya Jagadisha Hare), praises Narasimha's nails as the supreme weapon, neither weapon nor non-weapon — fulfilling another clause of Hiranyakashipu's boon-circumvention. The verse encapsulates the entire avatar's purpose in a single line of devotional wonder. (Sources: Bhagavata Purana 7.9.36; Jayadeva Gita Govinda Dasavatara stotra; Hindupedia Narasimha.)

seoFaqs.question: When should the Prahlada Stuti be recited for maximum benefit?
seoFaqs.answer: Narasimha Jayanti — the appearance day of Lord Narasimha — falls on Vaishakha Shukla Chaturdashi (April-May, the day before Vaishakha Purnima) and is the principal annual occasion for reciting the Prahlada Stuti. Holika Dahan and Holi (Phalguna Purnima, March) commemorate Prahlada's rescue from the burning of Holika and are also major occasions. Daily recitation is recommended on Saturdays (some traditions associate Narasimha with Saturday for protection from Shani), every Ekadashi, the entire Vaishakha month (Vishnu's month of avatara appearances), and during Bhagavata-saptaha. Devotees seeking protection from enemies, freedom from fear, removal of paranoid attacks, and unwavering faith amid persecution recite forty-day mandalas. (Sources: Drikpanchang Narasimha Jayanti; Hindupedia Holi origin; Bhagavata calendar.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Prahlada Stuti promise according to Vaishnava tradition?
seoFaqs.answer: Vaishnava tradition holds that recitation of the Prahlada Stuti grants nirbhayata (fearlessness — the supreme Prahlada quality), protection from enemies even when they hold worldly power, immunity from poison, fire, drowning, falling, weapons and animal attacks (the very dangers Prahlada survived), the awakening of unwavering bhakti to Vishnu, success in adverse environments, the conversion of hostile family or workplace relationships, and ultimately the highest liberation of becoming the Lord's eternal companion (parshada) in Vaikuntha. The stuti is especially recommended for those facing persecution for their faith, for children to develop fearless devotion, and for anyone in seemingly impossible adversity who needs Narasimha's intervention. (Sources: Bhagavata Purana phalashruti; Narasimha Tapaniya Upanishad; Vaishnava acharya tradition.)
